mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which mechanically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS growth greater than zero is not followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of out-of-sample months, or the mean forward return conditional on positive growth is not significantly above zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling three-month window signals deteriorating labor demand and incoming recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce negative 60-day forward returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or the Sharpe ratio of the signal over triggered periods is above -0.2.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a rising federal funds rate and a month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates that monetary tightening is successfully constraining consumer demand, and this interaction generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days beyond the independent effects of either variable.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month and RSAFS decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction term in a bivariate regression does not significantly improve prediction over main effects alone (F-test p > 0.05), or the joint condition fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days specifically during periods when supply chains are normalizing after a disruption, because output gains translate into revenue recovery without input cost spirals; outside this regime the relationship is weak or nil.
failure_condition: During identified post-supply-shock recovery months, positive month-over-month INDPRO changes are not followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the mean conditional return is not significantly above zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in monthly retail sales (RSAFS) reflects robust consumer demand that immediately boosts near-term earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as the market rapidly prices the demand signal.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS changes are not followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 58% of out-of-sample observations.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals an economy-wide contraction in aggregate demand and corporate revenues, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative GDPC1 growth prints are not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4.0 percent signals a tight labor market that generates wage-price spirals and invites restrictive monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE readings below 4.0% are not followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous month-over-month increase in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) captures stagflationary dynamics where monetary policy is constrained and corporate margins are compressed; this joint condition predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days beyond the main effects of either series.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month and UNRATE increases month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction term in a bivariate regression does not carry a negative coefficient significant at the 5% level, or the joint condition fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered months.
